title: Efficient Attention Variants
summary: O(n^2) self-attention is the bottleneck for long sequences. Sparse attention (attend to subset of positions), linear attention (kernel approximation to avoid n^2), Flash Attention (hardware-aware exact attention that is faster in practice). Critical for production deployment with long inputs.
key_insights: Standard attention: O(n^2 * d_k) — quadratic in sequence length
Sparse attention (Longformer, BigBird): attend to local window + global tokens. O(n * w) where w = window size
Linear attention (Performer, Linformer): approximate softmax(QK^T) via kernel trick. O(n * d_k)
Flash Attention: not a different algorithm — exact standard attention but with tiling/recomputation to minimize GPU memory transfers. 2-4x faster in practice
For long sequences (n > 4096), some form of efficient attention is required for practical deployment

mathematical_core.standard: O(n^2 * d_k) compute, O(n^2) memory for attention matrix
mathematical_core.sparse: O(n * w * d_k) where w = local window size + number of global tokens
mathematical_core.linear: Attention(Q,K,V) approx phi(Q) * (phi(K)^T * V) — reorder to avoid n^2 matrix. O(n * d_k^2)
mathematical_core.flash: Exact standard attention. Tiled computation avoids materializing full n^2 matrix in HBM. Same result, faster execution.

notes: Learning plan Week 4/7 topic. Relevant when sequence length exceeds ~4096 tokens.
